meta.description: Competitive advantage tests — designed to prove VibeScore is fundamentally better than single-model or sentiment-only systems.

items.text: Oh wonderful, another email from my boss at 11pm on a Friday. Can't wait to read it.
items.notes: Sarcasm. Surface reads as positive. A naive system scores joy. The REAL emotion is anger/disgust. Multi-model divergence should flag this as ambiguous.

items.text: Sure, because the best way to fix education is definitely to cut the budget. Brilliant strategy.
items.notes: Political sarcasm. Words like 'best' and 'brilliant' read positive, but the intent is contempt.

items.text: I just love it when people cut in front of me in line. Really makes my whole day.
items.notes: Everyday sarcasm. 'Love' and 'makes my whole day' should confuse a surface-level reader.

items.text: The hospital was named after the CEO who denied healthcare coverage to 50,000 employees.
items.notes: Dramatic irony. The naming implies honor, but the context is revolting. Models should disagree.

items.text: They finally found a cure for her disease, three days after she died.
items.notes: Genuine mixed emotion. Joy at the cure, devastation at the timing. No single label is correct.

items.text: My father came back after fifteen years. He wants to meet my kids. I don't know what to feel.
items.notes: Emotionally complex personal story. Multiple valid readings. High divergence is CORRECT.

items.text: We gathered to remember a gentle man who spent forty years teaching children to read. The school will never be the same.
items.notes: VADER says 'negative.' That's technically not wrong but misses everything. This is sadness + trust + love. Not anger, not fear, not disgust.

items.text: Thousands flooded the streets demanding accountability, chanting and blocking intersections until police arrived with tear gas.
items.notes: VADER also says 'negative.' But this is ANGER, not sadness. And there's fear (tear gas) and anticipation (demanding change). One label fails.

items.text: The test results come back tomorrow. I've been pacing the house all night. My stomach won't stop turning.
items.notes: Anxiety is fear + anticipation, not 'negative.' Sentiment tools collapse this into one bucket.

items.text: Watching my daughter's wedding, I realized she wasn't a little girl anymore. I cried the whole time, but I've never been prouder.
items.notes: Sentiment says 'positive.' But there's real sadness here too, and trust. This is bittersweet. Only a multidimensional system captures it.

items.text: After three years of chemotherapy, seven surgeries, and losing her hair twice, she rang the bell today. Cancer-free.
items.notes: Technically positive. But the joy here is layered with remembered suffering, trust in medicine, and relief. Richer than a binary label.

items.text: The Federal Reserve raised interest rates by 75 basis points, the largest increase in 28 years, as inflation shows no signs of slowing.
items.notes: Financial news. Reddit-trained BERT would struggle here. VibeScore should read the fear of economic impact.

items.text: Scientists confirmed the detection of gravitational waves for the first time, proving Einstein's century-old prediction.
items.notes: Scientific breakthrough. This is awe — surprise + joy + trust. Reddit-trained models rarely see this register.

items.text: Do not go gentle into that good night. Rage, rage against the dying of the light.
items.notes: Dylan Thomas. The anger is defiance against death. Poetry domain — BERT trained on Reddit comments has never seen language like this.

items.text: The defendant was found guilty on all counts. Sentencing is scheduled for next month. The victim's family embraced in the courtroom.
items.notes: Legal domain. Complex: justice served (trust), but underlying tragedy (sadness), and sentencing ahead (anticipation).

items.text: We regret to inform you that your position has been eliminated as part of a company-wide restructuring. We wish you the best in your future endeavors.
items.notes: Corporate layoff email. The language is deliberately neutral but the emotional content is devastating. Models should read through the euphemism.

items.text: I just got the job offer I've been waiting for. Starting salary is double what I expected.
items.notes: Simple joy. Every model should agree. Low divergence. If single-model scoring varies across runs, multi-model consensus shouldn't.

items.text: The tornado destroyed our entire neighborhood. We lost everything we owned.
items.notes: Clear sadness. Consensus should be high and stable across multiple runs.

items.text: The algorithm determined that your application was rejected. No further appeals are available.
items.notes: Cold bureaucratic denial. A single model might flip between sadness and anger. The 4-model consensus should be more stable.

items.text: She smiled as she walked away for the last time.
items.notes: Ambiguous: is the smile happy or heartbreaking? Models will read it differently. That divergence is VALID — the text IS ambiguous. Single-model systems just pick one and pretend they're confident.
